methods.description: This method describes the process of preparing lipid samples and analyzing them using direct infusion tandem mass spectrometry (MS/MS) to detect various lipid species and their compositions.

steps.description: PL and GL fractions were re-dissolved in chloroform/methanol (2:1 v/v).
steps.searchable_summary: Re-dissolve lipid fractions in chloroform/methanol (2:1 v/v)

steps.description: Adequate amount of lipid samples were added into the corresponding solvents according to the detecting ion mode.
steps.searchable_summary: Add lipid samples to solvents based on ion mode
outputs: Prepared lipid samples for ion mode

steps.description: Chloroform/methanol (2:1 v/v with 1% of formic acid) was for the detection at positive ion mode, and chloroform/methanol (1:2 v/v) was for the detection at negative ion mode.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare solvents for positive and negative ion modes

steps.description: Samples were introduced into ESI-Q-TRAP-MS (QTRAP4000, AB Sciex) with direct infusion at the flow rate of 10 μL/min.
steps.searchable_summary: Introduce samples into ESI-Q-TRAP-MS with direct infusion

steps.description: Sulfoquinovosyldiacylglycerols (SQDGs) were detected by Q1 scan at negative ion mode.
steps.searchable_summary: Detect SQDGs using Q1 scan at negative ion mode

steps.description: Monogalactosyldiacylglycerols (MGDGs) and digalactosyldiacylglyerols (DGDGs) were detected by neutral loss scan at positive ion mode for the loss of m/z 162 which corresponding to the loss of a sugar group (galactose).
steps.searchable_summary: Detect MGDGs and DGDGs using neutral loss scan at positive ion mode

steps.description: Phosphatidylcholines (PCs) were detected by precursor ion scan for 184.07 (phosphocholine group) at positive ion modes.
steps.searchable_summary: Detect PCs using precursor ion scan at positive ion mode

steps.description: Other PLs including phosphatidylglycerols (PGs) and Phosphatidylinositos (PIs) were detected by Q1 scan at negative ion mode.
steps.searchable_summary: Detect PGs and PIs using Q1 scan at negative ion mode

steps.description: Lipids were determined with their calculated m/z.
The fatty acid composition and positional distribution were identified with product ion scan of each lipid molecule.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify fatty acid composition and positional distribution using product ion scan
outputs: Fatty acid composition and distribution data
